meta.title: DBX-DEA-5: Lakehouse Foundations, Incremental Ingestion, and Workflows

domains.name: ELT with Spark SQL and Python

questions.stem: A team wants to use a storage architecture that keeps open object storage files while still adding table reliability features such as ACID transactions and schema enforcement. Which technology on Databricks best fits that need?

options.text: Parquet files in a plain data lake

questions.explanation: Delta Lake is correct because it layers transaction logs, schema enforcement, and time travel on open files in cloud object storage. The other options do not provide the full transactional table layer the scenario requires.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which workspace object is designed to manage reusable code, dashboards, notebooks, and folders for collaborative development?
questions.explanation: The workspace is the collaborative surface for notebooks, files, and other development assets. A catalog governs data objects, a metastore stores Unity Catalog metadata, and cluster policies constrain compute settings.
questions.reference: Databricks Certified Data Engineer Associate Exam Guide (July 25, 2025)

questions.stem: A bronze table stores raw JSON from many devices and a silver table standardizes types, removes corrupt rows, and deduplicates records. Which medallion layer is being described by the silver table?

options.text: Landing layer for unchanged source files
Validated and cleaned data ready for downstream modeling
A temporary shuffle layer created by Spark

questions.explanation: Silver data is the validated and conformed layer used after raw ingestion and before final business presentation. The other answers describe bronze, gold, or unrelated Spark internals.
questions.reference: Databricks Certified Data Engineer Associate Exam Guide (July 25, 2025)

questions.stem: Which statement about Delta Lake time travel is correct?

options.text: It requires copying old versions into a separate history database
It lets users query prior table versions or timestamps while retention permits
It works only for streaming tables, not batch tables
It replaces the need for checkpoints in Structured Streaming

questions.explanation: Time travel allows reads of earlier Delta table versions or timestamps as long as retained history exists. It does not depend on a separate history database and is unrelated to streaming checkpoints.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A new engineer needs a SQL endpoint for BI dashboards without managing Spark clusters directly. Which compute option is the best fit?
questions.explanation: SQL warehouses are built for SQL analytics and dashboard workloads. The other options either target engineering jobs or are unrelated to interactive BI querying.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which statement about Photon is most accurate for the associate exam scope?

options.text: Photon is a governance feature in Unity Catalog
Photon is the vector search engine for GenAI workloads
Photon is a high-performance query engine that can accelerate SQL and DataFrame workloads
Photon is the notebook version-control service

questions.explanation: Photon is Databricks’ optimized execution engine for many SQL and DataFrame operations. The other answers confuse it with unrelated products.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A team needs to share a Delta table with another organization without copying the data into the recipient account. Which feature should they consider first?
questions.explanation: Delta Sharing is designed for secure cross-platform data sharing without creating a full copy for each consumer. The other features address ingestion, remote development, or ML lifecycle tasks.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which statement best describes schema evolution in Delta Lake?

options.text: It rejects any write where the source has extra columns
It automatically changes partition values for old rows
It can add compatible columns when explicitly enabled during writes or merges
It converts all columns to STRING to avoid failures

questions.explanation: Schema evolution allows compatible schema changes such as adding columns when the write or merge enables it. By default extra columns are rejected, and Delta does not rewrite all columns as STRING.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: What is the main reason a data engineer would partition a large Delta table by a frequently filtered date column?

options.text: To reduce data scanned for queries that filter on that column
To avoid needing file compaction forever

questions.explanation: Partitioning can improve pruning for queries that filter on the partition column, reducing scanned data. It does not eliminate storage cost, transaction logs, or maintenance needs.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which statement best contrasts a lakehouse with a traditional data lake?

options.text: A lakehouse adds table management and reliability features on top of object storage
A lakehouse stores only unstructured data
A lakehouse cannot support machine learning workloads

questions.explanation: The lakehouse model keeps low-cost object storage but adds management features such as transactions, governance, and performance optimizations. The other statements are incorrect limitations.
questions.reference: Databricks Certified Data Engineer Associate Exam Guide (July 25, 2025)

questions.stem: A PySpark transformation must return all rows where `status` equals `ACTIVE` and `country` equals `US`. Which expression is valid?
questions.explanation: PySpark column expressions use bitwise operators such as `&` with parentheses. Python `and` does not work on Column objects, and the remaining options are invalid syntax.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which SQL function is most appropriate for turning an `ARRAY<STRING>` into multiple rows, one element per row?
questions.explanation: `explode` expands array elements into separate rows. The other functions aggregate, replace null handling, or create structs instead of row expansion.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A pipeline must combine daily sales tables from two regions that have identical schemas. Which operation is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: A union appends rows from datasets with compatible schemas. Joins combine columns based on keys, which is not what the scenario asks.
questions.reference: Databricks Certified Data Engineer Associate Exam Guide (July 25, 2025)

questions.stem: Which statement about user-defined functions on Databricks is most accurate for basic ETL work?

options.text: UDFs should usually be preferred over built-in functions because they always optimize better
Built-in Spark SQL functions are generally preferred when they can express the logic
UDFs can only be written in Scala, not Python
UDFs automatically make all transformations vectorized

questions.explanation: Built-in functions are usually preferred because they are easier for Spark to optimize and often perform better. The other statements overstate or misstate UDF behavior.
questions.reference: Databricks Certified Data Engineer Associate Exam Guide (July 25, 2025)

questions.stem: A SQL query needs to replace `NULL` values in `discount_pct` with 0. Which expression is simplest?
questions.explanation: `NVL` (or `COALESCE`) is used to substitute a value when an expression is null. The other options are not valid null-replacement expressions.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which join returns only rows from the left table that do not have a matching key in the right table?
questions.explanation: A left anti join keeps unmatched left-side rows only. Left semi keeps matched rows, full outer keeps matched and unmatched from both sides, and cross join ignores match keys.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A table contains one row per order and a nested struct column `shipping_address.city`. Which syntax selects the city in Spark SQL?
questions.explanation: Dot notation selects a field from a struct in Spark SQL. The other syntaxes do not match standard struct-field access.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A data engineer must compute total revenue by customer. Which clause groups rows before aggregation?
questions.explanation: `GROUP BY` defines aggregation groups. Ordering, distribution, and limiting do not perform semantic aggregation by customer.
questions.reference: Databricks Certified Data Engineer Associate Exam Guide (July 25, 2025)

questions.stem: Which higher-order function is best for keeping only positive elements from an integer array?
questions.explanation: `filter` returns the subset of array elements that satisfy a predicate. `transform` changes each element, `flatten` removes one nesting level, and `aggregate` reduces to a single value.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A source column contains timestamps as strings like `2026-06-01 14:30:00`. Which function should a data engineer use to convert them to timestamp values?
questions.explanation: `to_timestamp` converts formatted strings into timestamp values. The other functions serve unrelated purposes.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Select TWO statements that correctly describe a Delta `MERGE INTO` operation.

options.text: It can update matched target rows based on source data
It can insert new rows when no target match exists
It always requires the target table to be partitioned
It can be used only from Python, not SQL

questions.explanation: `MERGE INTO` supports matched updates and unmatched inserts, making it useful for upserts. It does not require partitioning and is available in SQL as well as programmatic APIs.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which command most directly registers a DataFrame as a temporary view for SQL queries in the same session?
questions.explanation: `createOrReplaceTempView` creates a session-scoped temporary view. `saveAsTable` writes a managed table, while the other commands serve different purposes.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A team needs to write a DataFrame to an existing Delta table while preserving old rows and adding only new rows. Which write mode should they use?
questions.explanation: Append adds new rows without replacing existing data. Overwrite would replace data, and the remaining modes do not satisfy the stated requirement.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: When should a data engineer most likely use a window function instead of a simple `GROUP BY`?

options.text: When the result should preserve individual rows while also computing values like rank or running totals
When all duplicate rows must be removed permanently
When converting CSV files to Delta tables

questions.explanation: Window functions compute analytics such as rank and running totals while keeping row-level detail. The other answers are unrelated tasks.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A JSON payload includes many optional fields and occasionally adds new keys. Which file format and table strategy on Databricks is generally the strongest fit for reliable incremental analytics?

options.text: Raw text files queried directly with no schema
CSV files loaded into temporary views only
Delta tables populated after parsing the JSON

questions.explanation: Parsing JSON into Delta tables provides stronger schema management, reliability, and downstream performance than querying raw text forever. The other options are poor fits for robust analytics pipelines.
questions.reference: Databricks Certified Data Engineer Associate Exam Guide (July 25, 2025)

questions.stem: A team ingests files that arrive continuously in cloud storage and wants scalable file discovery, schema inference, and checkpointed incremental processing. Which Databricks feature should they choose first?
questions.explanation: Auto Loader is built for incremental file ingestion with scalable discovery patterns and checkpointed streaming semantics. The other options are unrelated to ingestion.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Why is a checkpoint location important in Structured Streaming?

options.text: It tracks streaming progress and state so the query can recover correctly
It forces every micro-batch to overwrite the target
It is required only for batch jobs

questions.explanation: Checkpointing stores offsets and state metadata used for recovery and exactly-once style progress management where supported. It is not for dashboards and is not a batch-only concept.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which output mode is valid for many aggregate streaming queries when updated results should be emitted as aggregates change over time?
questions.explanation: `update` is a Structured Streaming output mode for supported queries where changed aggregate rows are emitted. The other terms are Delta maintenance features, not streaming output modes.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A streaming source may deliver late events. What is the main purpose of a watermark on event time?

options.text: To define how long the engine should wait for late data before trimming state

questions.explanation: Watermarks help bound state and decide how much lateness is tolerated for event-time operations. They do not encrypt data or convert types.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which statement best compares `COPY INTO` and Auto Loader?

options.text: `COPY INTO` is often convenient for idempotent SQL-based loading of a bounded set of files, while Auto Loader is stronger for ongoing scalable ingestion
Auto Loader can load only CSV, while `COPY INTO` supports all formats
`COPY INTO` is required for all streaming tables

questions.explanation: `COPY INTO` is useful for straightforward repeated loads of files, whereas Auto Loader is designed for large-scale continuous discovery and ingestion. The other statements are incorrect.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A team reads from Kafka in Structured Streaming and wants to write exactly one output record per unique event id. Which approach is most directly aligned with that goal?

options.text: Use `dropDuplicates` with the event id, usually together with event-time handling when needed
Convert the stream to pandas before writing

questions.explanation: Deduplication by event id is the direct Spark operation for removing duplicate events in a stream. The other choices are unrelated or unsafe.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: What is a micro-batch in Structured Streaming?

options.text: A small increment of new data processed as one streaming trigger
A cluster type reserved for notebooks
A Unity Catalog object used for lineage

questions.explanation: Structured Streaming commonly processes data in trigger-based micro-batches. The other answers describe unrelated concepts.
questions.reference: Databricks Certified Data Engineer Associate Exam Guide (July 25, 2025)

questions.stem: A source system lands daily full snapshots, but the downstream silver table should contain only the latest version of each business key. Which technique is most appropriate?

options.text: Use `MERGE INTO` on the business key
Use only `UNION ALL` forever with no deduplication
Write every snapshot to a different dashboard

questions.explanation: A merge keyed by the business identifier supports upsert-style maintenance of the latest record set. The other options do not maintain a clean current-state table.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Select TWO characteristics of Auto Loader schema evolution support.

options.text: It can rescue unexpected data into a rescued data column for later inspection
It can infer schema changes for arriving files over time when configured appropriately
It requires manually listing every new file path in code
It stores progress only in BI metadata tables

questions.explanation: Auto Loader can capture unexpected fields and evolve schemas depending on configuration. It does not require manual listing of every path and does not keep progress in BI metadata tables.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A streaming query reads from a Delta table and writes to another Delta table. Which capability allows efficient incremental downstream processing from table changes?

options.text: Change data feed when enabled on the source table

questions.explanation: Delta change data feed exposes row-level changes that downstream consumers can process incrementally. The other features do not provide data-change streams.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which Databricks feature is used to orchestrate scheduled notebook, Python, SQL, and JAR tasks as jobs?
questions.explanation: Databricks Workflows orchestrates scheduled and dependency-aware jobs across tasks. The other features cover governance, execution acceleration, or source control integration.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A workflow contains several tasks where task C should run only if both A and B succeed. What should the engineer configure?

options.text: Task dependencies for C on both A and B

questions.explanation: Task dependencies express execution order and success prerequisites inside a workflow DAG. The other options are unrelated.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: What is a key advantage of using a job cluster instead of an always-running all-purpose cluster for scheduled ETL?

options.text: Job clusters can reduce idle cost because they are created for the run and terminated afterward
Job clusters cannot access Unity Catalog
Job clusters can run only SQL tasks

questions.explanation: Ephemeral job clusters often reduce idle spend for scheduled pipelines. The other statements are false restrictions.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A production workflow should retry a flaky API ingestion task automatically before the whole pipeline is marked failed. Which job setting is most relevant?
questions.explanation: Retry settings control how failed tasks are reattempted in workflows. The other settings do not implement failure recovery behavior.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which statement about Delta Live Tables or Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines is most accurate at a high level?

options.text: They are designed to help define managed, declarative data pipelines with built-in expectations and operational features
They replace all need for SQL warehouses
They are only for serving ML models

questions.explanation: Managed declarative pipelines provide orchestration, quality controls, and operational support for data pipelines. The other statements are inaccurate.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A team wants to promote notebook code through pull requests and keep changes synced with a remote Git provider. Which Databricks capability helps most directly?
questions.explanation: Databricks Repos integrates workspace development with Git-based version control. The other options are for data/ML workloads, not source control.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which result best indicates a healthy bronze-to-silver production pattern?

options.text: Raw source files are edited in place after every downstream error
The silver layer applies validations and standardization before trusted downstream use
Gold tables are used only for landing unchanged raw payloads
Every task writes directly to dashboards instead of tables

questions.explanation: A healthy medallion pipeline uses silver for cleaned and standardized data before downstream consumption. The other patterns are poor engineering practices.
questions.reference: Databricks Certified Data Engineer Associate Exam Guide (July 25, 2025)

questions.stem: In Unity Catalog, which securable is the top container in the common hierarchy that then contains schemas?
questions.explanation: A catalog contains schemas, which in turn contain tables, views, and other objects. The other options are lower-level securables.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A user can query a table only if they have the required privileges through a direct grant or a group. Which statement about explicit deny rules in Unity Catalog is correct?

options.text: Unity Catalog supports `DENY` and it overrides grants
Unity Catalog has no explicit `DENY`; access is grant-based

questions.explanation: Unity Catalog uses grant-based authorization and does not provide an explicit deny privilege. The remaining options describe behavior that Unity Catalog does not implement.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog

questions.stem: A data engineer needs to control access to cloud storage paths used by managed ingestion and tables in Unity Catalog. Which securable is designed for that purpose?
questions.explanation: External locations govern access to referenced cloud storage paths under Unity Catalog. SQL warehouses and dashboard folders do not serve that storage-governance role.
questions.reference: Databricks docs: Delta Lake, Auto Loader, Workflows, and Unity Catalog
